info.name: IntelliDraft API
info.description: IntelliDraft backend API. Every document type (BRD, RFP, SOW, NIT, BOQ, NDPR, NFA, ARB, Proposal, Tech Spec, Scope) generates its own sections from its template and is styled with the Adani template. Set the {{document_type}} variable to switch types. Preview is asynchronous — poll /preview/html (or /preview/status) until status=ready.

exec: // Automatically add x-functions-key header if api_key variable is set.
// For local dev (run_server.py) leave api_key empty — Flask ignores the header.
// For Azure Functions deployment set api_key to the function host key.

variable.description: Azure Functions host key. Leave empty for local Flask server.
Chat session ID. Auto-set by POST /chat/init.
Human-readable project code (e.g. AESL-001). Used for ?code= lookup.
Celery task ID for LibreOffice HTML preview conversion. Auto-set by GET /preview/html when pending.
Document type to generate. One of: BRD, RFP, SOW, Proposal, Technical Specification, Scope, NDPR, NFA, NIT, BOQ, ARB — case-insensitive; long form e.g. 'Request for Proposal (RFP)' also works.

request.description: Lightweight liveness probe. Zero DB cost. Returns {status: 'ok'}.
Opens the interactive API reference HTML page.
DEV ONLY — wipes and recreates the SQLite database. Use for a clean slate during development. NOT safe in production (no auth guard).

exec: // Clear all saved IDs after DB reset

request.description: Returns all form field definitions for the Create Project form.
Upload a PPTX/DOCX/PDF/XLSX file. Returns document_id saved as collection variable. Max 50 MB.

formdata.description: Select your PPTX, DOCX, PDF, or XLSX file here

request.description: Get full parsed document metadata. Uses document_id from Upload.
Get lightweight document status (document_id, filename, file_type, status).
AI Populate button. Reads uploaded document(s) and extracts all 15 project form fields. Takes 30–60 s. Returns extracted{} + missing_required[].

Create a draft project without requiring all mandatory fields.

**OPTIONAL: Pass project_id (client-generated UUID) for idempotency.**
Frontend can call `crypto.randomUUID()` and include it in the body. On retry/autosave race, the same UUID returns 200 with the existing project — no duplicate created.

Returns 409 if project_code is already used by another project.

body.raw:
{
  "project_id": "{{project_id}}",
  "_comment": "project_id is OPTIONAL \u2014 pass a client-generated UUID (crypto.randomUUID()) for idempotent creation. Leave blank to have server generate one.",
  "project_name": "AESL Data Foundation",
  "project_code": "AESL-DF-001",
  "business_unit": "IT",
  "business_priority": "Critical",
  "document_type": "{{document_type}}"
}

request.description:
Save project to DB with full validation. All 12 marked fields required. Returns project_id.

⚠️ Returns 422 if required fields missing. Returns 409 if project_code already in use.

body.raw:
{
  "business_unit": "AESL",
  "project_name": "Smart Meter Analytics",
  "project_code": "AESL-2025-042",
  "problem_statement": "Manual meter reading is error-prone and costly, leading to billing disputes.",
  "project_objective": "Automate meter data collection with real-time analytics dashboard.",
  "stakeholders": [
    { "name": "Srinivas Gutta", "designation": "Project Manager" },
    { "name": "Ali Imam", "designation": "Technical Lead" }
  ],
  "start_date": "2025-04-01",
  "end_date": "2025-12-31",
  "as_is_processes": "Field engineers visit each meter monthly for manual reading.",
  "proposed_solution": "Deploy IoT smart meters with a central analytics platform integrated with SAP.",
  "technical_landscape": "Azure IoT Hub, Power BI, SAP ERP integration via REST API.",
  "business_priority": "Critical",
  "constraints": "Budget capped at 5 Crores. No disruption to existing billing cycle.",
  "risks": "Device connectivity in remote areas. Data privacy regulations.",
  "estimated_cost_crores": "4.5",
  "document_type": "{{document_type}}",
  "output_format": "Word (.docx)",
  "document_ids": ["{{document_id}}"]
}

exec: console.log('Project code already in use:', pm.response.json().error);

request.description: List all projects (most recent first). Enable query params to filter by name/code or status.
Get full project including all fields, stakeholders, doc IDs, and lifecycle metadata.

⚠️ DEPRECATED — use PATCH /api/projects/{{project_id}} instead.

PUT and PATCH do exactly the same thing (partial field update). PUT is kept for backwards compatibility but will be REMOVED in a future release.

Response includes X-Deprecated header and a 'deprecated' field in the JSON body.

Partial update — only the fields present in the body are written. Used for the frontend's autosave while filling the form.

⚠️ Returns 409 if the new project_code is already in use by another project.
Returns project meta at the top level (document_type, output_format, template_id, status) plus the 'ingested' form fields and 'derived' AI fields.

Save changes made to project ingested fields (from the project data page, Ingested tab).

**BUG FIX:** `Content-Type: application/json` header is NOT required — the server parses JSON regardless. This was the root cause of the "draft save not working" report.

Supported fields: project_name, business_unit, project_code, problem_statement, project_objective, as_is_processes, proposed_solution, technical_landscape, stakeholders, start_date, end_date, business_priority, constraints, risks, estimated_cost_crores.

Returns 409 if `project_code` conflicts with another project.
AI generates all 12 derived fields from project data + attached documents. Takes 30–90 s. Call GET /projects/{id}/data afterwards to read the results.

Pre-flight check before enabling the Generate button.

Checks all required fields are filled without starting generation.

Required fields: project_name, problem_statement, project_objective, as_is_processes, proposed_solution, technical_landscape.

Returns:
  valid: true|false
  ready_to_generate: true|false
  missing_required: [...field names...]
  message: human-readable summary

Save manual changes to AI-derived fields (from the project data page, Derived tab).

**BUG FIX:** `Content-Type: application/json` header is NOT required.

Supported fields: current_challenges, to_be_process, success_criteria, business_requirements, functional_requirements, non_functional_requirements, industry_benchmarks, workflow, analytics_requirements, systems_involved, data_sources, constraints_dependencies.

Pass `"mark_as_generated": true` to set `derived_generated_at` timestamp.
Permanently delete a project and its derived data. Returns 204 No Content.

Exact project_code lookup — resolves a human-readable code to the UUID project_id.

Use when you have the code (e.g. from a shared link or URL) but need the UUID for subsequent API calls.

Example: GET /api/projects?code=AESL-001

Returns { projects[], count }. Check projects[0].project_id for the UUID.

Start generation from a saved project. Body may override the type via "document_type" (defaults to the project's stored type). Supported: BRD, RFP, SOW, Proposal, Technical Specification, Scope, NDPR, NFA, NIT, BOQ, ARB — case-insensitive; long form e.g. 'Request for Proposal (RFP)' also works. Each type generates its OWN sections/formatting from its template.

⚠️ DEPRECATED — use POST /api/generate/project/{{project_id}} instead.

This legacy endpoint requires a raw document_id and bypasses the project database. Use POST /api/generate/project/{{project_id}} which reads all project context from DB.

Response includes X-Deprecated header and a 'deprecated' field in the JSON body.
Poll every 3 s to track progress. Check section statuses: pending → generating → completed/failed. Also saves the first section_id for use in section endpoints.
Get a single section with all versions and all comments.
Add a user review comment to a section. Save the returned comment_id for use in Regenerate.
Regenerate section incorporating the comment. Creates a new version. Omit comment_id to regenerate from scratch.
Accept a specific version. This version will be used in the final export.

Return a single section with all versions, comments, and current content.

Used by the inline editor to load the Markdown content before the user edits.

Response: `{ section_id, section_title, status, current_version, versions[], comments[] }`

Manually overwrite a section with new Markdown content.

Creates a new SectionVersion (`trigger_type: manual_edit`), marks it `is_accepted: true`, updates the version_hash cache, and invalidates the LibreOffice preview cache.

This is the endpoint called when the user edits a section inline in the document preview or via the sidebar drawer.

Response: `{ version: { version_number, content, trigger_type, word_count, ... }, message }`

body.raw:
{
  "content": "## Introduction

This section has been manually edited to include updated project scope and revised KPIs.

- KPI 1: Reduce manual effort by 40%
- KPI 2: Real-time data latency < 5 seconds
- KPI 3: 99.9% system uptime"
}

request.description:
Return the assembled document as markdown for on-screen rendering in the Chat UI.

**Local dev:** `blob_url` is null — use the `export_urls.docx` path to download.
**Azure (prod):** `blob_url` is populated after the first export call — use it to open/embed the DOCX directly from Azure Blob Storage.

Response: `{ job_id, status, document_type, project_name, sections[], markdown, export_urls, blob_url }`

- `markdown` — full assembled document string, render with marked.js or similar
- `sections[]` — individual sections with title, content, status, word_count
- `export_urls` — relative paths for DOCX / PDF / Markdown download
- `blob_url` — Azure Blob URL (prod only, null in local dev)

exec: console.log('Blob URL:', j.blob_url || '(local mode — use export_urls)');

request.description: Download the assembled document. Change format= to pdf or md if needed.

item.name: 🖥️ HTML Preview (LibreOffice)
item.description:
LibreOffice DOCX→HTML preview pipeline. First call triggers async Celery task; poll status until ready. Subsequent calls return cached HTML instantly.

The HTML preview has inline editing enabled — clicking a section heading in the preview opens a contenteditable editor directly in the document.

request.description: Get the rendered HTML preview. ASYNCHRONOUS: may return {"status":"pending", "poll_url":"...", "retry_after_ms":1500}. Keep re-GETting poll_url (this same endpoint in sync mode, or /preview/status?task_id=... in Celery mode) until {"status":"ready", "html":...}. Do NOT treat 'pending' as an error.

Poll Celery task status for HTML preview conversion.

Response: `{ status: 'pending' | 'ready' | 'error', html?, error? }`

Repeat every 2s until `status === 'ready'`. The `html` field contains the full LibreOffice HTML with inline-editing scripts injected.

exec: if (json.status === 'ready') console.log('Preview ready. HTML length:', json.html.length);

item.name: 📸 Version Snapshots
item.description:
Point-in-time document checkpoints. Save the current state of all accepted sections as a named snapshot, list all snapshots for a job, and restore any snapshot to roll back the document.

New in this sprint: full versioning + audit trail with trigger_type on every SectionVersion.

request.description:
Create a DocumentSnapshot capturing the current accepted (or latest) version of every section.

Returns `{ snapshot_id, label, section_count, created_at }`.

Use `label` to describe what this snapshot represents (e.g. 'After legal review', 'Pre-client review v1').

List all snapshots for a job, most recent first.

Response: `{ snapshots: [{ snapshot_id, label, trigger_type, created_at, section_count }], total }`

Restore a document to a saved snapshot.

Marks the referenced SectionVersion for each section as `is_accepted=true`, clears the accepted flag on other versions, and invalidates the preview cache.

Response: `{ restored_sections: [{ section_id, version_number }], snapshot_id }`

query.description: BRD | RFP | NIT | BOQ | NDPR | NFA | ARB

request.description:
List all document templates. Filter by document type with `?document_type=` query param.

**Short aliases supported:** BRD, RFP, SOW, NDPR, NFA, NIT, BOQ, ARB
**Full canonical names also work:** "Business Requirements Document (BRD)", "Request for Proposal (RFP)", etc.

Returns: `{ templates: [{ template_id, name, document_type, is_system, sections }] }`
Create a user-defined template with custom sections.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "AESL Custom BRD",
  "document_type": "{{document_type}}",
  "sections": [
    "Executive Summary",
    "Problem Statement",
    "Business Requirements",
    "Technical Requirements",
    "Implementation Plan"
  ],
  "description": "AESL internal BRD format",
  "system_instructions": "Always use formal tone. Include AESL branding. Use metric units."
}

request.description:
Force re-seed a system template from its JSON file in Data_Ingestion/templates/.

Use this after editing a template JSON file (brd, rfp, nit, boq, ndpr, nfa, arb) — the DB gets updated without a server restart.

Change 'brd' in the URL to the template id you want to reseed.

item.name: 💬 Chat Studio
item.description: Document Chat Studio — conversational UI for project setup and document generation. Flow: init session → send messages → upload docs via chat → AI generates document.

request.description: Create/resume a chat session. "document_type" selects the type (short or long form). Supported: BRD, RFP, SOW, Proposal, Technical Specification, Scope, NDPR, NFA, NIT, BOQ, ARB — case-insensitive; long form e.g. 'Request for Proposal (RFP)' also works.

Send a user message and receive an assistant response.

The assistant classifies intent and routes to the right action:
- 'generate the document' → triggers generation
- 'regenerate introduction' → regenerates a section
- 'I want to change the tone' → adds a comment and regenerates
- general questions → answered directly
Return the full ChatSession record including all messages, project_id, job_id, and current phase.
Upload a document from the chat UI. Parses the file, attaches it to the session's project, and returns a chat-style confirmation response.

formdata.description: Select your PPTX, DOCX, PDF, or XLSX file

exec: console.log('Document uploaded via chat. document_id:', json.document_id);

item.name: 🔁 Full Flow (Run in Order)
item.description: Run these requests in sequence to test the complete create → generate → export workflow.

request.description: Immediately persist AI-extracted fields to DB without validation. Creates a draft project so data survives a browser refresh.

exec: console.log('WARNING: project_code already in use —', pm.response.json().error);

body.raw:
{
  "project_objective": "Automate meter data collection with real-time analytics.",
  "stakeholders": [{ "name": "Srinivas Gutta", "designation": "Project Manager" }],
  "start_date": "2025-04-01",
  "end_date": "2025-12-31",
  "as_is_processes": "Field engineers visit each meter monthly for manual reading.",
  "proposed_solution": "Deploy IoT smart meters with a central analytics platform.",
  "technical_landscape": "Azure IoT Hub, Power BI, SAP ERP integration.",
  "business_priority": "Critical",
  "document_type": "{{document_type}}",
  "output_format": "Word (.docx)",
  "status": "ready"
}

request.description: Check all required fields are filled before generating. If valid=false, fill missing fields before proceeding.

Start generation from a saved project. Body may override the type via "document_type" (defaults to the project's stored type). Supported: BRD, RFP, SOW, Proposal, Technical Specification, Scope, NDPR, NFA, NIT, BOQ, ARB — case-insensitive; long form e.g. 'Request for Proposal (RFP)' also works. Each type generates its OWN sections/formatting from its template.

Get full document as markdown for on-screen rendering. Call after status=completed.

Render the markdown field in the UI. export_urls gives you DOCX/PDF/MD download links.
All registered users (feeds the Admin Panel).
Upsert by email. Roles: Admin | Project Manager | Contributor | Viewer.
5 system personas (PM, Technical Reviewer, BA, Compliance Officer, Financial Auditor) + caller's custom personas.
Custom persona owned by the caller (X-User-Email).
System personas cannot be edited (403).
Author shares a generated document. Sets job review_status=under_review. Save review_id from the response.
Reviews the caller requested — reviewers with statuses (shared/reviewing/accepted/rejected/revision_requested), comment counts, days since shared.
Documents shared WITH the caller, including who shared and my_status.
Everything for the review screen: meta, reviewers, document sections (for anchoring), threaded comments, cached AI summaries. Opening as a reviewer flips shared→reviewing.
section_id optional (document-level if omitted). For replies pass parent_id.
text: author-only edit. resolved: anyone can toggle.
Author-only; replies are removed too.
Returns {summary, section_comments:[{section_id, section_title, comment}]}. NOT persisted — reviewer picks what to keep.
Persists the AI comments the reviewer selected (source=ai, tagged with persona).
Author: persona-wise AI summaries of ALL reviewer comments. Cached.
Latest summary per persona, no LLM call.
accepted | rejected | revision_requested. Rolls up job review_status: any rejected→rejected, any revision→revision_requested, all accepted→approved.
Stamps last_renotified_at on pending reviewers (notification stub).
Author: converts the review comment into the standard section-regeneration flow → new SectionVersion. section_id only needed for un-anchored comments.
